title: Common fixed phrases
intro: These phrases are used as fixed chunks in English. One word cannot be swapped for a synonym without changing the meaning or sounding wrong. (Verb + preposition combinations such as depend on, take part in are covered in **Reference → Dependent prepositions**.)

phrases.example: They went abroad in search of sunshine.
It is no longer available.
In fact, I've never been there.
Of course, you're right.

phrases.phrase: as long as / so long as
phrases.example: As long as you're careful, it's fine.

phrases.meaning: part of a group (e.g. one of the best)
phrases.example: This is one of the best films I've seen.

phrases.phrase: in doing so / by doing so
phrases.example: In doing so, they saved time.
In spite of the rain, we went out.
In addition to English, she speaks Spanish.
At first, I didn't like him.
As well as cooking, she cleans.
It was the first time I had flown.

phrases.meaning: no later than the end of
phrases.example: By the end of the day, we were tired.
With the rise of the internet, habits changed.
They studied chimpanzees in the wild.
She is in charge of the project.
I'm in favour of the plan.
I'm speaking on behalf of the team.
As a result of the delay, we missed the flight.
For the sake of clarity, I'll repeat it.
At least we tried.
We left at once.
Call me as soon as you arrive.
So far, so good.
The hotel is cheap. On the other hand, the location is poor.
I'll make dinner. In the meantime, you can relax.

phrases.meaning: used to introduce a new or related topic
phrases.example: By the way, have you heard from Sarah?

phrases.meaning: to explain something differently or more clearly
phrases.example: He's frugal. In other words, he's careful with money.

phrases.meaning: typically or as a general rule
phrases.example: On average, people check their phones 100 times a day.
In general, technology has improved our lives.
We eat out from time to time.
Saving money will help you in the long run.

phrases.phrase: to some extent / to a certain extent
phrases.meaning: partly or to a limited degree
phrases.example: To some extent, I agree with the proposal.
Many animals are at risk of extinction.
Make sure your passport is up to date.
Giving up now is out of the question.
Needless to say, everyone was shocked by the news.
It goes without saying that hard work is essential.
In terms of quality, this product is excellent.
Thanks to modern medicine, many diseases are curable.

phrases.meaning: as stated by / in accordance with
phrases.example: According to experts, climate change is accelerating.
With regard to your email, I have attached the file.

phrases.phrase: on no account / under no circumstances
phrases.example: On no account should you open the door to strangers.
All in all, the trip was a great success.
The match was cancelled on account of the rain.

phrases.phrase: in spite of the fact that
phrases.example: We went out in spite of the fact that it was raining.
In contrast to his brother, he prefers tea.
We use butter, as opposed to margarine.
The changes are in line with the new policy.
For the time being, we'll stay here.
Growth came at the expense of the environment.
They met with a view to reaching an agreement.
We use plastic in place of glass.
Everyone is welcome regardless of their background.
The data was collected for the purpose of research.

phrases.meaning: the real fact; what is actually the case
phrases.example: The truth of the matter is that we underestimated the cost.
Why don't you have a go? I'm sure you'll do well.
I've never tried sushi, but I'll give it a go.

phrases.meaning: referring to all the things just mentioned (relative clause)
phrases.example: She gave three reasons, all of which were convincing.

phrases.meaning: to so great a degree (often followed by that)
phrases.example: Prices rose to such an extent that many could not afford to buy.

make_do.example: I made a mistake in the calculation.
She needs to do her homework.
We need to make a decision soon.
They are doing research on climate change.
We are making progress on the project.
I'll do my best to help.
It's easy to make friends at school.
